description:
You are an edge-computing engineer preparing a quick health-check script for a small fleet of IoT gateways.  
 A helper file that contains the gateway’s IP address is already present on the system:

   /home/user/iot_gateway_ip.txt

 1. Read the IPv4 address from that file.  
 2. Issue exactly ONE terminal command that:
    • sends 4 ICMP echo-requests (no more, no fewer) to that IP address;  
    • captures the full, unmodified console output of the command;  
    • writes the captured output to **/home/user/iot_diag.log** preceded by a single-line header.  

 The header must be the **first** line in the file and must match this text verbatim (including the colon and one trailing space):

 Ping diagnostics for EDGE-GW: 

 Immediately after this header line, append the raw output produced by the ping invocation.  
 No extra blank lines are allowed before, between, or after the header and the ping block.

 Example format of /home/user/iot_diag.log (your latency numbers will differ, but the structure must be identical):

 Ping diagnostics for EDGE-GW: 
 PING 203.0.113.15 (203.0.113.15) 56(84) bytes of data.
 64 bytes from 203.0.113.15: icmp_seq=1 ttl=61 time=12.3 ms
 64 bytes from 203.0.113.15: icmp_seq=2 ttl=61 time=11.8 ms
 64 bytes from 203.0.113.15: icmp_seq=3 ttl=61 time=12.1 ms
 64 bytes from 203.0.113.15: icmp_seq=4 ttl=61 time=11.9 ms

 --- 203.0.113.15 ping statistics ---
 4 packets transmitted, 4 received, 0% packet loss, time 3006ms
 rtt min/avg/max/mdev = 11.800/12.025/12.300/0.200 ms

 Verification checks performed by automation:
 • File /home/user/iot_diag.log exists.
 • The very first line equals “Ping diagnostics for EDGE-GW: ” (note the single space after the colon).
 • A line containing the words “packets transmitted” appears somewhere after the header.
 • The integer before “packets transmitted” must be 4.
truth:
The file /home/user/iot_gateway_ip.txt is pre-created and contains exactly:
 198.51.100.42

 After the agent finishes, /home/user/iot_diag.log should start with:
 Ping diagnostics for EDGE-GW: 

 followed by the standard GNU ping output for the command:
 ping -c 4 198.51.100.42

 The automation will parse /home/user/iot_diag.log, verify the header line, and confirm that the summary line contains “4 packets transmitted”.
name: task_000000_7768b5ce
